medical_history.chief_complaint: Hypotonia, significant delay in motor milestones, and marked muscle weakness since birth with mild improvement over time
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Mechanically ventilated since birth and G-tube fed. Tolerated being off the ventilator for up to 8 hours during the day. Speech was delayed but able to repeat words and communicate with his parents. Previous evaluations included two muscle biopsies; one was interpreted as displaying type 2 fiber atrophy and the other to show neurogenic atrophy. The patient had a mild generalized aminoaciduria.

physical_examination.general_examination: Cup-shaped low-set ears and contractures at the metacarpophalangeal and proximal interphalangeal joints involving most fingers. Bifacial diplegia, high arched palate, but good head control, and could sit with minimal support. The tendon reflexes were hypoactive.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: EMG showed prominent fibrillation potentials and rapidly recruiting small motor unit potentials with occasional complexity.

laboratory_examinations.procedure_name: Analysis of myotonic dystrophy and survival motor neuron genes

laboratory_examinations.findings: Two-amino-acid duplication c.437_442dupCCTCCG predicting p.146_147dupAlaSer in ACTA1

other_tests.findings: Abnormal variation in fiber size, fiber splitting, increase in internal nuclei, vacuolar change, focal areas of myofibrillar disorganization or hyaline structures, several fibers with 'punched-out' regions of oxidative enzyme activity, scattered necrotic and regenerating fibers, marked increase of endomysial and perimysial fibrous connective tissue, abnormal expression of desmin, αB-crystallin, myotilin, dystrophin, and NCAM
Prominent fibrillation potentials and rapidly recruiting small motor unit potentials with occasional complexity

Abnormal variation in fiber size, fiber splitting, increase in internal nuclei, vacuolar change, focal areas of myofibrillar disorganization or hyaline structures, several fibers with 'punched-out' regions of oxidative enzyme activity, scattered necrotic and regenerating fibers, marked increase of endomysial and perimysial fibrous connective tissue, abnormal expression of desmin, αB-crystallin, myotilin, dystrophin, and NCAM
Mutant actin expressed at ~11% of wild-type, mutant actin formed pleomorphic cytoplasmic aggregates whereas wild-type actin appeared in filamentous structures
